metadata.title: Reviewed but out-of-scope group-generation-adjacent resources
metadata.description: Resources that were explicitly reviewed after the broader ecosystem pass, but not merged into the ranked Awesome Group Generators dataset because they are sales-gated, LMS-native documentation rather than a portable tool, app-store-only without a verified workflow, duplicate/fork variants, unavailable, or primarily research/reference material.
metadata.policy: These entries are evidence-tracked so future reviewers do not re-review the same adjacent resources. Promote an item into data/tools.json only after a directly usable generator, open demo, app workflow, or runnable implementation has been reviewed enough to assign the normal feature/rating fields.

items.name: FeedbackFruits Group Formation
items.whyOutOfScope: The public page is a commercial/LMS solution with Book a demo/get-started sales flow and no independently reviewable generator workflow.
items.publicEvidence: The page claims balanced, diverse, high-performing student teams using smart/customizable criteria and positions Group Formation as one FeedbackFruits learning tool.
items.notes: Strong conceptual fit, but keep unranked unless a public demo or institution-account workflow can be reviewed.

items.name: Moodle Group Formation plugin
items.whyOutOfScope: Installable Moodle plugin rather than a standalone reviewed generator; requires a Moodle site/course context.
items.publicEvidence: The plugin directory describes automated group formation in Moodle using questionnaire/preference-style data.
items.notes: Useful LMS-native lead; revisit if testing a Moodle plugin environment becomes part of the methodology.

items.name: Moodle Auto-create groups
items.whyOutOfScope: Native Moodle documentation, not an independently reviewable tool entry; no standalone generator URL or runnable workflow outside Moodle.
items.publicEvidence: Moodle docs cover automatic group creation and member allocation workflows inside a Moodle course.
items.notes: Mention as ecosystem coverage rather than ranked public tool.

items.name: Canvas random group assignment
items.whyOutOfScope: Canvas/Instructure documentation for a built-in LMS workflow; requires Canvas course/admin context and is not a portable group generator.
items.publicEvidence: Documentation describes randomly assigning students to groups/group sets, including course-section handling.
items.notes: Important classroom ecosystem capability, but not comparable to ranked self-serve tools.

items.name: OptAssign
items.whyOutOfScope: The reviewed source is a ScienceDirect article/abstract, not a live or runnable tool that can be rated.
items.publicEvidence: The paper describes a web-based tool for assigning students to groups based on project/topic preferences and constraints.
items.notes: Good reference for assignment-method coverage; not enough public tool access for data/tools.json.

items.name: OVO Slack RandomCoffee
items.whyOutOfScope: Reviewed as an AWS Lambda/API Gateway fork of pyslackrandomcoffee; functionality substantially duplicates the ranked pyslackrandomcoffee entry.
items.publicEvidence: README states it is a fork of tjansson60/pyslackrandomcoffee, matches Slack channel members for coffee, posts results to Slack, and avoids recent repeats via channel history.
items.notes: Keep as a known deployment variant; do not rank separately unless it diverges materially.

items.name: coffee_roulette_pairs
items.whyOutOfScope: Direct GitHub/API review returned 404 during this pass, so the implementation could not be verified from source.
items.publicEvidence: Search snippets describe an R package that generates random coffee-roulette pairs from CSV input and can exclude unwanted/recent pairs.
items.notes: Promote only if the repository becomes publicly accessible again or an archived package source is found.

items.name: Macarons for Slack
items.whyOutOfScope: Commercial Slack Marketplace app requiring Slack installation/sign-in; no self-serve generator or workspace workflow was tested.
items.publicEvidence: Marketplace page says it automatically pairs random channel members for coffee/video calls and lets admins control timing, odds, and channel lotteries.
items.notes: Relevant coffee-chat product, but track outside ranked list until an install/workspace review is available.

items.name: LEAD.bot Virtual Coffee Chat
items.whyOutOfScope: Commercial Slack/Teams employee-connection platform; public page is marketing/install flow rather than a reviewed generator.
items.publicEvidence: The page claims automated virtual coffee matching, introductions, scheduling, feedback, smart matching, and cross-team programs for Slack and Teams.
items.notes: Strong adjacent product; keep unranked like other enterprise coffee-chat platforms.

items.name: PerfectTablePlan speed networking
items.whyOutOfScope: Already tracked in data/related-commercial-platforms.json as a non-ranked commercial speed-networking seating rotation product.
items.publicEvidence: Public page claims generation of multiple seating arrangements/rotations with no repeated companions.
items.notes: No additional ranked action needed unless a public generator/demo is reviewed.

items.name: SportsVybe Team Generator
items.whyOutOfScope: App Store listing only; no installed app workflow was reviewed in this pass.
items.publicEvidence: Listing describes sports team generation with skill ratings and team-balancing/captain-style workflows.
items.notes: Potential future mobile-app review candidate.

items.name: CSPLib Social Golfers Problem models
items.whyOutOfScope: Canonical problem/model reference, not an end-user group generator.
items.publicEvidence: CSPLib provides the Social Golfers Problem definition and MiniZinc/model resources for no-repeat group scheduling.
items.notes: Useful background for social-golfer/repeat-minimization methods.

items.name: MiniZinc social golfers example
items.whyOutOfScope: Documentation/example model for MiniZinc, not a ready group-generation product.
items.publicEvidence: MiniZinc handbook uses the social golfers problem as a set-constraint modelling example.
items.notes: Reference for people building solvers; not a ranked tool.

items.name: Hexaly Social Golfer template
items.whyOutOfScope: Optimization vendor template/reference rather than an organizer-facing generator reviewed with sample data.
items.publicEvidence: Template describes modelling the Social Golfer Problem to maximize socialization/minimize repeated meetings across weeks.
items.notes: Relevant algorithm reference; keep separate from end-user tools.

items.name: metalevel Social Golfer resources
items.whyOutOfScope: Reference/resource page for the formal Social Golfer Problem, not a generator workflow.
items.publicEvidence: The page collects background and methods for the Social Golfer Problem.
items.notes: Useful as a bibliography/reference candidate if the site grows a research section.

items.name: DrawTeams.com

items.publicEvidence: Session review recorded that the domain redirected to parked/lander HTML despite search snippets for an old team generator.
items.notes: Found while reviewing session logs/compaction summaries for missed rejected candidates.

items.name: Group Picker Team Mixer
items.whyOutOfScope: The deployment returned HTTP 402 “Deployment Paused” during browser and fetch review, so no generator workflow could be verified.
items.publicEvidence: Candidate was described as category-balanced team mixing with file upload/print/copy, but live page showed only “This deployment is temporarily paused.”
items.notes: Revisit if the deployment comes back online.

items.name: Group Picker homepage
items.whyOutOfScope: The homepage also returned HTTP 402 “Deployment Paused,” so the simpler Group Picker workflow could not be reviewed.
items.publicEvidence: Live page showed only a paused deployment message from the host.
items.notes: Same unavailable product as Group Picker Team Mixer.

items.name: TeamMaker Hackathon Tool
items.category: stale demo / open-source hackathon team former
items.whyOutOfScope: The marketing page loads and describes score/rule-based hackathon team formation, but its live demo links point to a dead Heroku app returning “No such app.”
items.publicEvidence: Public page describes user database, score analysis, autosort teams, rules such as max users/desirable frontend engineers, and warning when balanced teams are impossible.
items.notes: Could be promoted if the frontend/backend source is run and reviewed or a live demo returns.

items.name: goodMatches
items.whyOutOfScope: App Store-only racket-sports matchup rotation generator; no installed app workflow was tested.
items.publicEvidence: Listing describes rating-based balanced matchup sequences for 5–20 players, singles/doubles, fair resting rotation, mid-session changes, invite codes, rating updates, and rankings.
items.notes: Relevant to sports rotations; keep out of ranked data until app-tested.
